**`internal/fec/fec.go`** — Config(struct)

```go
1	package fec
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/binary"
5		"fmt"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// maxShards is the Reed-Solomon field limit: RS over GF(2^8) admits at most 256
10	// shards total (data + parity) per coding group.
11	const maxShards = 256
12	
13	// lenPrefixLen is the width of the big-endian length header prepended to each
14	// data payload before it is padded to the group's uniform shard length. It lets
15	// the decoder recover the exact original byte length after Reed-Solomon
16	// reconstruction, since RS operates only on fixed-length shards. A uint32 prefix
17	// covers any datagram this codec will ever wrap.
18	const lenPrefixLen = 4
19	
20	// ShardFramingOverhead is the fixed per-shard framing this codec adds on top of the
21	// caller's payload before Reed-Solomon coding — the big-endian length prefix. A
22	// parity shard's wire payload is ShardFramingOverhead + the group's largest coded
23	// data payload, so the multipath Bind uses it (with the outer parity/data header
24	// delta) to size the FEC MTU penalty (T24).
25	const ShardFramingOverhead = lenPrefixLen
26	
27	// Config parameterizes a Reed-Solomon FEC group: DataShards (N) opaque DATA
28	// frames are grouped and protected by ParityShards (K) parity frames, so the
29	// receiver recovers up to K arbitrary losses per group. Deadline bounds grouping
30	// latency: a group that does not fill to N within Deadline is flushed early over
31	// however many data frames it holds, so the parity is never delayed unboundedly
32	// waiting for a group to fill.
33	type Config struct {
34		// DataShards is N, the maximum number of DATA frames grouped before parity is
35		// emitted. Must be >= 1.
36		DataShards int
37		// ParityShards is K, the number of parity frames emitted per group and the
38		// maximum number of per-group losses the receiver can recover. Must be >= 1.
39		ParityShards int
40		// Deadline bounds the grouping latency: an open, partially-filled group is
41		// flushed (parity emitted over its current data frames) once this much time
42		// has elapsed since its first frame. Must be > 0.
43		Deadline time.Duration
44	}
45	
46	// Validate reports whether the configuration is a usable Reed-Solomon FEC ratio,
47	// failing fast on an out-of-range shard count or a non-positive deadline. It is the
48	// exported form of the same invariant NewEncoder/NewDecoder enforce, so a caller (the
49	// multipath Bind, T24) can reject a bad ratio before constructing either.
50	func (c Config) Validate() error { return c.validate() }
51	
52	func (c Config) validate() error {
53		if c.DataShards < 1 {
54			return fmt.Errorf("fec: DataShards must be >= 1, got %d", c.DataShards)
55		}
56		if c.ParityShards < 1 {
57			return fmt.Errorf("fec: ParityShards must be >= 1, got %d", c.ParityShards)
58		}
59		if c.DataShards+c.ParityShards > maxShards {
60			return fmt.Errorf("fec: DataShards+ParityShards must be <= %d, got %d", maxShards, c.DataShards+c.ParityShards)
61		}
62		if c.Deadline <= 0 {
63			return fmt.Errorf("fec: Deadline must be > 0, got %s", c.Deadline)
64		}
65		return nil
66	}
67	
68	// GroupID identifies one FEC coding group. The encoder assigns group ids
69	// monotonically; the decoder buckets received shards by this id.
70	type GroupID uint32
71	
72	// Shard is the closed sum over the two kinds of frame an FEC group emits: a
73	// DataShard wrapping one opaque payload, and a ParityShard carrying redundancy.
74	// The unexported marker prevents other packages from introducing new kinds.
75	type Shard interface {
76		// GroupID returns the coding group this shard belongs to.
77		GroupID() GroupID
78		isShard()
79	}
80	
81	// DataShard is one opaque DATA payload admitted into a group, tagged with its
82	// coding coordinates so the decoder can place it. It carries no group cardinality
83	// (M), because a data frame is emitted immediately on admission — before the
84	// group's final size is known — to keep datapath latency low; the decoder learns
85	// M from any ParityShard of the group.
86	type DataShard struct {
87		Group   GroupID
88		Index   int    // position within the group, 0..M-1
89		Payload []byte // the opaque original bytes (never inspected)
90	}
91	
92	// GroupID returns the shard's coding group.
93	func (d DataShard) GroupID() GroupID { return d.Group }
94	func (DataShard) isShard()           {}
95	
96	// ParityShard is one Reed-Solomon parity frame for a group. It carries DataCount
97	// (M) and its Payload length equals the group's uniform shard length, so a single
98	// received parity frame tells the decoder both the group cardinality and the
99	// shard geometry it needs to reconstruct losses.
100	type ParityShard struct {
101		Group     GroupID
102		Index     int    // parity position, 0..K-1; RS shard index is DataCount+Index
103		DataCount int    // M: number of data shards this parity protects
104		Payload   []byte // parity bytes; len == group shard length
105	}
106	
107	// GroupID returns the shard's coding group.
108	func (p ParityShard) GroupID() GroupID { return p.Group }
109	func (ParityShard) isShard()           {}
110	
111	// Recovered is a data payload the decoder reconstructed from parity — one that
112	// was NOT directly received. Directly-received data frames are never echoed back
113	// as Recovered; the caller already holds them.
114	type Recovered struct {
115		Group   GroupID
116		Index   int
117		Payload []byte
118	}
119	
120	// encodeDataShard renders one payload as a fixed-length RS shard: a big-endian
121	// uint32 length prefix followed by the payload, right-padded with zeros to
122	// shardLen. shardLen is guaranteed >= lenPrefixLen+len(payload) by the caller.
123	func encodeDataShard(payload []byte, shardLen int) []byte {
124		s := make([]byte, shardLen)
125		binary.BigEndian.PutUint32(s, uint32(len(payload)))
126		copy(s[lenPrefixLen:], payload)
127		return s
128	}
129	
130	// decodeDataShard inverts encodeDataShard, returning the exact original payload.
131	// It fails fast on a length prefix that overruns the shard, which would indicate
132	// corruption or a decoder/encoder mismatch rather than a recoverable condition.
133	func decodeDataShard(shard []byte) ([]byte, error) {
134		if len(shard) < lenPrefixLen {
135			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: shard too short for length prefix: %d bytes", len(shard))
136		}
137		n := int(binary.BigEndian.Uint32(shard[:lenPrefixLen]))
138		if n > len(shard)-lenPrefixLen {
139			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: shard length prefix %d overruns shard payload %d", n, len(shard)-lenPrefixLen)
140		}
141		out := make([]byte, n)
142		copy(out, shard[lenPrefixLen:lenPrefixLen+n])
143		return out, nil
144	}
```

```go
21	// Fetch GETs baseURL (e.g. "http://127.0.0.1:9095/metrics") with client and
22	// parses the response into an Exposition. It is the scrape helper future e2e
23	// tests use to drive assertions against a running endpoint.
24	func Fetch(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, baseURL string) (Exposition, error) {
25		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, baseURL, nil)
26		if err != nil {
27			return Exposition{}, fmt.Errorf("metrics: build scrape request: %w", err)
28		}
29		resp, err := client.Do(req)
30		if err != nil {
31			return Exposition{}, fmt.Errorf("metrics: scrape %q: %w", baseURL, err)
32		}
33		defer func() { _ = resp.Body.Close() }()
34		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
35			return Exposition{}, fmt.Errorf("metrics: scrape %q: status %s", baseURL, resp.Status)
36		}
37	
38		// v0.66's TextParser requires an explicit name-validation scheme; the
39		// zero-value parser leaves it unset and panics. UTF8Validation matches the
40		// promhttp exposition this endpoint serves.
41		parser := expfmt.NewTextParser(model.UTF8Validation)
42		families, err := parser.TextToMetricFamilies(resp.Body)
43		if err != nil {
44			return Exposition{}, fmt.Errorf("metrics: parse exposition: %w", err)
45		}
46		// Drain any residue so the connection can be reused.
47		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
48		return Exposition{families: families}, nil
49	}
```

```go
1	package fec
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/binary"
5		"fmt"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// maxShards is the Reed-Solomon field limit: RS over GF(2^8) admits at most 256
10	// shards total (data + parity) per coding group.
11	const maxShards = 256
12	
13	// lenPrefixLen is the width of the big-endian length header prepended to each
14	// data payload before it is padded to the group's uniform shard length. It lets
15	// the decoder recover the exact original byte length after Reed-Solomon
16	// reconstruction, since RS operates only on fixed-length shards. A uint32 prefix
17	// covers any datagram this codec will ever wrap.
18	const lenPrefixLen = 4
19	
20	// ShardFramingOverhead is the fixed per-shard framing this codec adds on top of the
21	// caller's payload before Reed-Solomon coding — the big-endian length prefix. A
22	// parity shard's wire payload is ShardFramingOverhead + the group's largest coded
23	// data payload, so the multipath Bind uses it (with the outer parity/data header
24	// delta) to size the FEC MTU penalty (T24).
25	const ShardFramingOverhead = lenPrefixLen
26	
27	// Config parameterizes a Reed-Solomon FEC group: DataShards (N) opaque DATA
28	// frames are grouped and protected by ParityShards (K) parity frames, so the
29	// receiver recovers up to K arbitrary losses per group. Deadline bounds grouping
30	// latency: a group that does not fill to N within Deadline is flushed early over
31	// however many data frames it holds, so the parity is never delayed unboundedly
32	// waiting for a group to fill.
33	type Config struct {
34		// DataShards is N, the maximum number of DATA frames grouped before parity is
35		// emitted. Must be >= 1.
36		DataShards int
37		// ParityShards is K, the number of parity frames emitted per group and the
38		// maximum number of per-group losses the receiver can recover. Must be >= 1.
39		ParityShards int
40		// Deadline bounds the grouping latency: an open, partially-filled group is
41		// flushed (parity emitted over its current data frames) once this much time
42		// has elapsed since its first frame. Must be > 0.
43		Deadline time.Duration
44	}
45	
46	// Validate reports whether the configuration is a usable Reed-Solomon FEC ratio,
47	// failing fast on an out-of-range shard count or a non-positive deadline. It is the
48	// exported form of the same invariant NewEncoder/NewDecoder enforce, so a caller (the
49	// multipath Bind, T24) can reject a bad ratio before constructing either.
50	func (c Config) Validate() error { return c.validate() }
51	
52	func (c Config) validate() error {
53		if c.DataShards < 1 {
54			return fmt.Errorf("fec: DataShards must be >= 1, got %d", c.DataShards)
55		}
56		if c.ParityShards < 1 {
57			return fmt.Errorf("fec: ParityShards must be >= 1, got %d", c.ParityShards)
58		}
59		if c.DataShards+c.ParityShards > maxShards {
60			return fmt.Errorf("fec: DataShards+ParityShards must be <= %d, got %d", maxShards, c.DataShards+c.ParityShards)
61		}
62		if c.Deadline <= 0 {
63			return fmt.Errorf("fec: Deadline must be > 0, got %s", c.Deadline)
64		}
65		return nil
66	}
67	
68	// GroupID identifies one FEC coding group. The encoder assigns group ids
69	// monotonically; the decoder buckets received shards by this id.
70	type GroupID uint32
71	
72	// Shard is the closed sum over the two kinds of frame an FEC group emits: a
73	// DataShard wrapping one opaque payload, and a ParityShard carrying redundancy.
74	// The unexported marker prevents other packages from introducing new kinds.
75	type Shard interface {
76		// GroupID returns the coding group this shard belongs to.
77		GroupID() GroupID
78		isShard()
79	}
80	
81	// DataShard is one opaque DATA payload admitted into a group, tagged with its
82	// coding coordinates so the decoder can place it. It carries no group cardinality
83	// (M), because a data frame is emitted immediately on admission — before the
84	// group's final size is known — to keep datapath latency low; the decoder learns
85	// M from any ParityShard of the group.
86	type DataShard struct {
87		Group   GroupID
88		Index   int    // position within the group, 0..M-1
89		Payload []byte // the opaque original bytes (never inspected)
90	}
91	
92	// GroupID returns the shard's coding group.
93	func (d DataShard) GroupID() GroupID { return d.Group }
94	func (DataShard) isShard()           {}
95	
96	// ParityShard is one Reed-Solomon parity frame for a group. It carries DataCount
97	// (M) and its Payload length equals the group's uniform shard length, so a single
98	// received parity frame tells the decoder both the group cardinality and the
99	// shard geometry it needs to reconstruct losses.
100	type ParityShard struct {
101		Group     GroupID
102		Index     int    // parity position, 0..K-1; RS shard index is DataCount+Index
103		DataCount int    // M: number of data shards this parity protects
104		Payload   []byte // parity bytes; len == group shard length
105	}
106	
107	// GroupID returns the shard's coding group.
108	func (p ParityShard) GroupID() GroupID { return p.Group }
109	func (ParityShard) isShard()           {}
110	
111	// Recovered is a data payload the decoder reconstructed from parity — one that
112	// was NOT directly received. Directly-received data frames are never echoed back
113	// as Recovered; the caller already holds them.
114	type Recovered struct {
115		Group   GroupID
116		Index   int
117		Payload []byte
118	}
119	
120	// encodeDataShard renders one payload as a fixed-length RS shard: a big-endian
121	// uint32 length prefix followed by the payload, right-padded with zeros to
122	// shardLen. shardLen is guaranteed >= lenPrefixLen+len(payload) by the caller.
123	func encodeDataShard(payload []byte, shardLen int) []byte {
124		s := make([]byte, shardLen)
125		binary.BigEndian.PutUint32(s, uint32(len(payload)))
126		copy(s[lenPrefixLen:], payload)
127		return s
128	}
129	
130	// decodeDataShard inverts encodeDataShard, returning the exact original payload.
131	// It fails fast on a length prefix that overruns the shard, which would indicate
132	// corruption or a decoder/encoder mismatch rather than a recoverable condition.
133	func decodeDataShard(shard []byte) ([]byte, error) {
134		if len(shard) < lenPrefixLen {
135			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: shard too short for length prefix: %d bytes", len(shard))
136		}
137		n := int(binary.BigEndian.Uint32(shard[:lenPrefixLen]))
138		if n > len(shard)-lenPrefixLen {
139			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: shard length prefix %d overruns shard payload %d", n, len(shard)-lenPrefixLen)
140		}
141		out := make([]byte, n)
142		copy(out, shard[lenPrefixLen:lenPrefixLen+n])
143		return out, nil
144	}
```

content.text:
All grounding checks are complete. Assessment against the rubric:

**Fine-grained / sequenced / testable** — three tasks, one per defect, each independently completable with operational acceptance (exact commands, fail-first tests, capture-logger assertions, table-driven cases). The T133→T134 edge is genuinely load-bearing: T133 adds tests to `internal/bind/probe_test.go`, and T134's `NewMultipath` signature change must then update every bind test file including those new tests — verified 9 device.go callers + 6 bind test files, matching the task text. T135 touches only `reloadWarnings`/`reload_test.go` (a different region of device.go than T134's call sites) — genuinely parallel.

**Grounded** — verified against source: `emitProbes` (probe.go:50) and the echo reflection (multipath.go:1693 — the task correctly flags the drifted line ref) are the only two uncounted tx write sites (`Send` :1784 and `fecFlushDeadline` :2018 both count); probes/echoes bypass Send, so no double-counting, and `txBytes` is an `atomic.Uint64` incremented outside `m.mu` — no locking hazard. `listenPath` (pathsock.go:35-43) does swallow the `listenOnDevice` error; returning the fallback fact keeps pathsock.go logging-free and testable via the existing `addPathListen`/`resolveDeviceBind` seams, and both fallback layers (unresolvable interface + setsockopt/CAP) are covered. `reloadWarnings` (device.go:549) omits exactly Scheduler/FEC/DNS/Bind; `Metrics` holds only `Listen` (applied by Reload), so the no-warn-on-Metrics and zeroed-copy instructions are sound. The T104 subtest exists and already asserts `delta > 0` via `t.Errorf`.

**Two precision gaps found** (planner-fixable, verdict → revise):

1. **T133 misses the in-code counter contract and docs-sync.** `peerPathState`'s comment (multipath.go:157-167) states txBytes "counts the DATA-frame wire bytes this path egresses on the Send hot path" and defines the data-thrift signal as "the backup path's Send count stays ~flat" — false after T133 (the standby's counter growing is the fix). The task directs only the internal/metrics help string. Also, the "flip" is a commentary update (the test's doc comment and Errorf refile-instructions), not an assertion inversion — and T133 uniquely lacks the AGENTS.md docs-sync clause T134/T135 carry, despite changing an operator-visible metric's semantics.

2. **T135's Bind either/or is a false dichotomy.** Bind lives at BOTH levels: top-level `c.Bind` default (normalize, config.go:841-843) AND per-path `p.Bind` with fallback (:847-849). Correct handling is both/and: per-path `l.Bind != d.Bind` comparison plus explicit handling of top-level `c.Bind` (own case or deliberate catch-all zeroing), else a top-level change produces a generic double-warning instead of one actionable message.

No user-only gaps; no new out-of-scope defects (D48's "refile" instruction is already satisfied — it is D48).

### Session summary
- **Did:** reviewed the emitted plan for goal G10 (T133/T134/T135 under M46) against D48/D52/D53, grounding every cited site in internal/bind/{probe,multipath,pathsock}.go, internal/device/{device,metrics}.go, internal/metrics, internal/config/config.go, and test/e2e/standby_liveness_test.go
- **Achieved:** verdict revise; mode B → json returned, no ledger writes; 2 criticisms / 0 new questions / 0 out-of-scope defects
- **Discovered:** the D48 fix sites are complete and hazard-free (only 2 uncounted tx writes; atomic counters); the T104 subtest already encodes the green assertion; Config.Bind exists at both top level and per-path (T135's either/or is factually wrong); the txBytes contract comment at multipath.go:157-167 will contradict the new wire-volume semantics and T133 does not cover it
- **Issues:** none — codegraph could not confirm whether README/design.md document tx_bytes semantics (markdown not indexed), so criticism 1 phrases docs-sync as a check-and-update clause

```json
{
  "summary": "Plan is fine-grained, correctly sequenced (T133→T134 justified by the NewMultipath signature change touching T133's new tests; T135 genuinely parallel), testable, and accurately grounded in the verified fix sites — but two precision gaps need one revision pass: T133 omits the contradicting txBytes contract comment (multipath.go:157-167) and the AGENTS.md docs-sync clause, and T135 poses Bind as per-path OR top-level when config.go has both.",
  "verdict": "revise",
  "new_questions": [],
  "criticism": [
    "T133: the peerPathState counter-contract comment at internal/bind/multipath.go:157-167 explicitly states txBytes 'counts the DATA-frame wire bytes this path egresses on the Send hot path' and defines the data-thrift signal as 'the backup path's Send count stays ~flat' — both false once probe/echo bytes are counted (the standby's counter growing is the very fix). Task item (3) directs only the internal/metrics help string; extend it to update this comment. Also add the AGENTS.md docs-sync clause T134/T135 both carry (check/update README.md and docs/design.md wherever wanbond_path_tx_bytes_total or the idle-standby tx symptom is documented) — T133 changes an operator-visible metric's semantics yet is the only task without it. Finally, clarify that 'flip the T104 subtest' means updating its stale repro commentary (the file doc-comment predicting failure and the t.Errorf refile-as-defect instructions): the subtest already asserts delta>0 via t.Errorf, so no assertion logic is inverted.",
    "T135: the Bind instruction poses a false dichotomy ('if per-path ... if a top-level section') — config.Config has BOTH: a top-level c.Bind global default (normalize, internal/config/config.go:841-843) AND per-path Path.Bind with fallback to it (:847-849). Rewrite as both/and: extend the same-name-path comparison to l.Bind != d.Bind (normalize resolves the default into every path, so this covers effective changes), AND explicitly handle top-level c.Bind (its own DeepEqual case, or deliberately zero it in the catch-all as covered-by-per-path) — otherwise a top-level bind change fires the generic catch-all warning in addition to, or instead of, an actionable bind-specific message."
  ],
  "defects": []
}
```
